violations.description: General employee practices. — --Food employee observed with soiled outer clothing. -Apron visibly dirty.

Preventing contamination from equipment, utensils, and wiping cloths. — --Cloth for wiping food spills was not stored in sanitizing solution (at proper concentration) between uses. -Sanitizer not made/available during service.

Refrigerated Storage. — --Refrigerator or cold storage was insufficient or ineffective at maintaining potentially hazardous food at a temperature of 41F or less. -Right side stainless refrigerator found with temperature of 44F. (Thermometer showed ~50F).

Sanitizing solution testing devices. — --Test strip or kit that accurately measures the sanitizing concentration was not provided. -Facility has chlorine test strips but is using Member's Mark Quat sanitizer.

Handwashing lavatories. — --Handwashing lavatory was not provided in sufficient number and/or not accessible to allow convenient use by employees. -Water pressure was not correct upon arrival, had to work with water lines and pump to get sink to work.
Handwashing, good hygienic practices (5 pts deducted)
Designed, constructed, installed, and maintained (2 pts deducted)
Cleaning frequency, maintained clean (1 pt deducted)
Thermometers provided, used, clean, and accurate (1 pt deducted)
Properly designed, located, constructed, operated, maintained, cleaned and chemical test kit provided (2 pts deducted)
Handwashing, good hygienic practices (5 pts deducted)
Available, adequate capacity, able to maintain proper temperature (4 pts deducted)
